rulings.Case Title: ISHAN BASU-KESSELMAN VS. GARUDA LABS, INC.; A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: SETTLEMENT COMPLIANCE HEARING
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025 Line 2. SETTLEMENT COMPLIANCE HEARING. The Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement) is CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 11, 2025, AT 9:00 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT. Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than February 3, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: 123 CORBETT, LP VS. TALBCO DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025 Line 2. PLAINTIFF 123 CORBETT, LP's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff 123 CORBETT, LP's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Moving party has established the discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant's objections are overruled. The court is not ruling on a protective order because no noticed motion for a protective order is before it. In any event, the record demonstrates the PMK deposition is properly conducted in person at Plaintiff's counsel's offices in San Francisco over two consecutive 8-hour days or until completed, whichever comes first. It's a deposition of a PMK, not Talbot. If Talbco designates Talbot its PMK, that's its choice. Defendant and Talbot have had plenty of time to arrange travel and childcare. The relevant work was performed here by Defendant and the case is properly venued here. The PMK deposition is properly conducted in person here. The Talbco Development Corporation PMK deposition shall take place in person at Plaintiff's counsel's offices in San Francisco over two consecutive 8-hour days or until completed, whichever comes first, forthwith, but in no event later than 5 calendar days from notice of this order. The court does not find Defendant Talbco Development Corporation acted with substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $3,420 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025 Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Trembach declaration shows that plaintiff's counsel has not met and conferred in compliance with CCP 430.41. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered immediately to comply with the code. Defendant Lowe's response to the complaint is now due by February 24, 2025. In addition, no party provided courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: AMUN-RE KING VS. MARLTON AFFORDABLE HOUSING ASSOCIATES, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025 Line 8. 2 - DEFENDANTs MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA XLIV A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, ELIJAH DONALDSON, AISHA FORKS,and MARLTON AFFORDABLE HOUSING ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Amended complaint filed. The court notes that this a landlord-tenant habitability case and such cases are heard in department 501 per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAMSARA INC. VS. MOTIVE TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025 Line 9. 2 - PLAINTIFF SAMSARA INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Off calendar. The case was declared complex and assigned to Judge Schulman in department 304 for all purposes. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
